title: Vanguard Renewables Opens River Falls RNG Facility
content:
Vanguard Renewables, a leader in organics recycling, has opened a new anaerobic digestion and advanced depackaging facility in River Falls, Wisconsin, to serve the Minneapolis-St. Paul region. The facility offers food and beverage companies a sustainable, turnkey solution for managing waste and complying with growing regulatory pressures, such as Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) and organics diversion requirements in Wisconsin and Minnesota.

Located at Peterson Family Dairy, the site can process up to 275 tons of food and beverage waste daily. It utilizes anaerobic digestion to convert this waste into 289,000 MMBtu of Renewable Natural Gas (RNG) annually. This RNG output is sufficient to power approximately 53,000 homes and businesses, and will specifically help power AstraZenecaâ€™s U.S. facilities as part of a procurement agreement. The project is part of Vanguard's joint venture with TotalEnergies and expands the company's presence in the Midwest.
author: Analysts Account
